name: MCPJungle
what_it_does: MCPJungle is a self-hosted MCP gateway that aggregates multiple MCP servers behind a single endpoint, enabling AI clients and agents to access all registered tools through one connection with canonical tool naming.
use_cases: Centralizing access to multiple MCP servers for Claude Desktop or Cursor without per-client configuration
Organizations deploying AI agents in production needing unified tool management and monitoring
Teams requiring a single MCP endpoint with security controls and privacy isolation across multiple backend servers
not_for: Users who only need one MCP server (no aggregation benefit)
Fully managed/cloud-hosted MCP infrastructure (MCPJungle is self-hosted only)
Non-technical users who cannot operate a server or Docker container
best_when: You manage several MCP servers and want AI clients to connect to a single endpoint, or you need centralized control and logging over agent tool access.
avoid_when: You need a SaaS/hosted solution, your setup only involves a single MCP server, or you require advanced RBAC and observability beyond basic bearer token auth.

auth.notes: Bearer token authentication for HTTP-based MCP servers. Custom HTTP header configuration per registered server. No built-in user auth layer for the gateway itself beyond token passing.

pricing.notes: MPL-2.0 licensed. Free to self-host. Optional PostgreSQL for enterprise deployments adds infra cost.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: CLI provides tool invocation feedback but gateway-level error propagation is not detailed in docs.
known_agent_gotchas: Canonical tool names use server__tool format which agents must learn or discover at runtime
Stateless vs stateful session mode trade-offs require explicit configuration tuning
stdio servers require Docker image with Node.js/Python variant for subprocess support
